allies.rationale_text: Scaffolding-educational supports divergent-vs-convergent-thinking by giving learners a staged way to expand and then synthesize understanding.
allies.source_quote: In learning, new concepts are typically introduced by presenting a complex idea broken down into smaller, simpler units (scaffolding/chunking), which facilitates both the focused work and the diffuse synthesis.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Divergent-vs-convergent-thinking conflicts with cognitive-biases when fast intuitive closure or familiar frames collapse the search space before real alternatives are generated.
structured_tensions.source_quote: Availability Heuristic and substitution bias halt the open-ended exploration of divergent thinking and prematurely drive toward a convergent, but potentially wrong, answer.

curation_notes.summary: Preserved only current-vocabulary first-order seams that were explicit or cleanly warranted by the raw source.
donor_drops: Did not keep donor-shaped or out-of-vocabulary relation surfaces; omission beat weak forcing.
open_questions: Higher-order candidates remain observational only and belong in later selective review rather than in this closure batch.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only for this model.
antagonists: Any broader antagonistic surface not preserved here was omitted because the current first-order vocabulary did not justify forcing it.
structured_tensions: Only explicit or cleanly source-supported tensions that survive current-vocabulary discipline were preserved.
